patch.value:
## Context

Project management is context management. Integration tests must prove package activation without consuming unrelated operator context.

## Scope

Isolate the package lifecycle smoke test and verify the full release contract.
Isolate package-install integration tests from the user's pm home

The package activation smoke test only redirected PM_GLOBAL_PATH, leaving HOME and XDG state shared with the operator. A real install test must be hermetic so it cannot read or mutate personal/global pm configuration.

The package install smoke test runs with temporary HOME, XDG_CONFIG_HOME, and XDG_DATA_HOME directories; all activation/generate/check assertions pass against pm CLI 2026.7.10; the full release gate passes in a clean environment.
Current origin/main is checked out; latest pm CLI dependency is installed; no unrelated working-tree changes exist.
Run the package-install test while a real ~/.pm-cli configuration exists and observe that only PM_GLOBAL_PATH is redirected.
Integration tests are fully isolated from operator home/config/data.
The test inherits HOME and XDG paths and can observe global state.
Prevents tests and review bots from coupling to or modifying private user configuration.

value.author: codex
value.text: Discovered while manually running the full release gate against real local pm data.

value.author: codex
value.text: Use temporary HOME and XDG roots rather than only PM_GLOBAL_PATH because package lifecycle state spans standard user directories.

value.author: codex
value.text: Package integration tests must isolate both product-specific globals and standard HOME/XDG locations to avoid privacy leaks and nondeterminism.

patch.value: 128 tests passed, package install/doctor/generate/check flows succeeded, npm audit found 0 vulnerabilities, pack dry-run succeeded, and pm-changelog reported the changelog current.
The package install integration test cannot read or write operator-level pm configuration and the complete release gate passes.
Replaced the package smoke test's product-specific global override with temporary HOME, XDG_CONFIG_HOME, and XDG_DATA_HOME roots shared across every spawned pm command.
Hermetic HOME/XDG isolation implemented and the complete release gate passed on pm CLI 2026.7.10.

value.author: codex
value.text: Greptile P2 accepted: spreading process.env could preserve an operator-exported PM_GLOBAL_PATH. The test now overrides PM_GLOBAL_PATH to a temporary path in addition to HOME and XDG directories.

value.author: codex
value.text: When constructing a hermetic child environment, overriding HOME/XDG is insufficient if product-specific path variables can be inherited; explicitly replace every supported storage-root override after spreading process.env.
